verses.text: David asks the LORD to contend with those who contend with him and to take up shield and spear in his defence. He makes God his champion. The wronged believer may commit his battle to the Lord.
David asks the LORD to contend with those who contend with him and to take up shield and spear in his defence. He makes God his champion. The wronged believer may commit his battle to the Lord.
David asks the LORD to contend with those who contend with him and to take up shield and spear in his defence. He makes God his champion. The wronged believer may commit his battle to the Lord.
He prays that those seeking his life be confounded and driven like chaff before the angel of the LORD. He leaves their pursuit to God. Vengeance is safest in the hands of the righteous Judge.
He prays that those seeking his life be confounded and driven like chaff before the angel of the LORD. He leaves their pursuit to God. Vengeance is safest in the hands of the righteous Judge.
He prays that those seeking his life be confounded and driven like chaff before the angel of the LORD. He leaves their pursuit to God. Vengeance is safest in the hands of the righteous Judge.
Though they hid a net for him without cause, he asks that ruin overtake them unawares, caught in their own snare. Malice recoils on itself. The pit dug for the innocent often swallows its digger.
Though they hid a net for him without cause, he asks that ruin overtake them unawares, caught in their own snare. Malice recoils on itself. The pit dug for the innocent often swallows its digger.
Then his soul shall rejoice in the LORD, who delivers the poor from those too strong for them. Rescue turns to praise. None is like God in saving the weak from the mighty.
Then his soul shall rejoice in the LORD, who delivers the poor from those too strong for them. Rescue turns to praise. None is like God in saving the weak from the mighty.
False witnesses rose against him, repaying his good with evil, to the spoiling of his soul. He suffered base ingratitude. To be wronged by those we helped is a bitter but not uncommon grief.
False witnesses rose against him, repaying his good with evil, to the spoiling of his soul. He suffered base ingratitude. To be wronged by those we helped is a bitter but not uncommon grief.
Yet when they were sick, David had mourned and fasted for them as for a friend or brother. He had loved his now-enemies. The godly return kindness for the very ones who later turn on them.
Yet when they were sick, David had mourned and fasted for them as for a friend or brother. He had loved his now-enemies. The godly return kindness for the very ones who later turn on them.
But in his adversity they gathered to mock, gnashing at him with hypocritical scorners. Their cruelty repaid his love. The world often rejoices at the stumbling of the righteous.
But in his adversity they gathered to mock, gnashing at him with hypocritical scorners. Their cruelty repaid his love. The world often rejoices at the stumbling of the righteous.
'Lord, how long wilt thou look on?' he cries, vowing to thank God in the great congregation once rescued. He longs for God to act. The believer's plea is mingled with the promise of public praise.
'Lord, how long wilt thou look on?' he cries, vowing to thank God in the great congregation once rescued. He longs for God to act. The believer's plea is mingled with the promise of public praise.
He asks that his deceitful foes not gloat, who plot against the quiet of the land and open wide their mouths against him. Their lies are laid before God. He trusts the Judge to silence the false.
He asks that his deceitful foes not gloat, who plot against the quiet of the land and open wide their mouths against him. Their lies are laid before God. He trusts the Judge to silence the false.
He asks that his deceitful foes not gloat, who plot against the quiet of the land and open wide their mouths against him. Their lies are laid before God. He trusts the Judge to silence the false.
'This thou hast seen, O LORD,' he says; 'keep not silence... judge me according to thy righteousness.' He appeals to the all-seeing God. What men hide, God has witnessed, and He will judge it justly.
'This thou hast seen, O LORD,' he says; 'keep not silence... judge me according to thy righteousness.' He appeals to the all-seeing God. What men hide, God has witnessed, and He will judge it justly.
'This thou hast seen, O LORD,' he says; 'keep not silence... judge me according to thy righteousness.' He appeals to the all-seeing God. What men hide, God has witnessed, and He will judge it justly.

He asks that his enemies not say, 'we have swallowed him up,' but be ashamed, while he speaks of God's righteousness all the day. Deliverance ends in praise. The vindicated soul gives glory not to itself but to God.
